subject: english
topic_title: Essay Writing: Types of Essays and Report Writing
learning_objectives: Identify and describe the main types of essays
Explain the structure of a formal report
Write minutes of a meeting correctly

sections.heading: Types of Essays
sections.content:
Essay writing is an art of communication that requires the acquisition of necessary skills.

1. Narrative – Tells a story or relates events as an eyewitness.
2. Descriptive – Describes a person, object, animal, scene, or incident.
3. Expository – Explains a topic or process in detail.
4. Argumentative – Presents a viewpoint to persuade the reader.
5. Creative Writing – Imaginative writing in various forms.

sections.heading: Formal Report Writing
sections.content:
A formal report may cover a robbery, accident, meeting proceedings, work-camp, or petition. It requires good knowledge of direct and indirect speech.

Format when reporting to a school principal:
[Class/Address]
[Date]
The Principal,
[Name of School],
[Location].
Sir/Ma,
[Subject Heading]
(Body of the report)
Signature
[Name and Role/Title]

sections.heading: Minutes of a Meeting
sections.content:
Minutes of a meeting are a record of events and discussions that took place at a meeting. Every meeting should have a prepared agenda – a list of items to be discussed.

A minutes report should include:
• Name of the club/organisation
• Date and venue of the meeting
• List of members present
• Items discussed under each agenda point
• Decisions or resolutions made
